Portfolio case file / NLP / Climate finance / Local LLM

Climate risk signals hidden in 10,814 annual reports

A reproducible NLP pipeline that turns Chinese A-share annual reports into evidence-backed TCFD disclosure intelligence across policy, market, and technology dimensions.

Finding: climate disclosure accelerates sharply after 2020. Policy and compliance language remains the dominant signal, while transition technology terms become increasingly visible in recent filings.

Evidence extracted from annual-report language

#1 Environmental Protection / Risk 12,906 records link this pair as risk language in management discussion. 12,906
#2 Energy Consumption / Reduction 7,881 records link this pair as transition and decarbonization activity. 7,881
#3 Phase-Out / Backward Capacity Phase-Out 6,372 records link this pair as recurring climate-disclosure language. 6,372

Engineering Quality

01 / Result

From filings to climate-risk evidence

The project turns annual-report text into a structured map of climate-risk language. It combines local LLM extraction, co-occurrence analysis, TCFD relevance evaluation, semantic clustering, and a public-safe interactive report.

Disclosure acceleration

71 -> 27,741

TCFD-related disclosures grew from 2000 to 2024; the latest five years account for 59% of all detected disclosures.

Policy-led signal

102,273

Policy and compliance language is the dominant disclosure pattern, while technology signals contribute 62,465 mentions and market signals 7,945.

Engineering proof

Local LLM + tests

The report is generated from a reproducible pipeline: streaming JSONL loading, structured validation, leakage checks, and an automated test suite.

The sunburst below shows the vocabulary hierarchy: TCFD dimension → semantic cluster → individual keyword. It is a compact view of the topic space discovered by the pipeline.

Dominant layer

Policy-first

Policy language contributes 102,273 disclosures, making compliance the clearest entry point into the taxonomy.

Transition layer

62,465

Technology terms cluster around efficiency, energy substitution, and operational retrofit.

Market layer

7,945

Market signals are smaller but connect risk language to price, finance, and volatility.

02 / System

A pipeline built for noisy disclosure text

Raw annual-report text moves through sampling, chunking, local LLM keyword extraction, co-occurrence analysis, TCFD scoring, and topic clustering. The public report is the final artifact, not a hand-made dashboard.

01

Sample reports

Load annual-report files and keep year-level provenance.

02

Split text

Convert long filings into bounded analysis chunks.

03

Extract terms

Use a local LLM to identify climate-related terms.

04

Build pairs

Count co-occurring terms that describe the same disclosure signal.

05

Score TCFD fit

Route evidence into policy, market, and technology dimensions.

06

Cluster topics

Aggregate similar language into readable chart structures.

Reusable pattern

Multi-dimensional routing, structured LLM outputs, concurrent batch processing, retry handling, and downstream analytics can transfer to other document-heavy domains.

Public-safe output

Company names are anonymized, chart data is embedded into a single HTML file, and a leakage scanner runs before publication.

03 / Findings

The signal is temporal, then linguistic

The strongest story is time: climate-related disclosures were sparse in the early 2000s and surged in the 2020s. The streamgraph lets you compare how policy, market, and technology language evolved.

Temporal signature

Post-2020 surge

Detected disclosures rise from 71 in 2000 to 27,741 in 2024. The latest five years contain 59% of detected disclosures.

Dimension balance

Policy / Tech / Market

102,273 policy, 62,465 technology, and 7,945 market disclosures are visible in the evaluated set.

Recent language graph

The most frequent co-occurrences reveal the vocabulary behind the trend: compliance pressure, energy reduction, and transition technology dominate the recent disclosure graph.

How to read it

Dense center, specific edges

Large nodes are recurring terms; edges show which concepts companies discuss together.

Pipeline refinement

10,814 reports → chunks → disclosures → dimensions. Click a link to view source contexts.

04 / Engineering

Turning stochastic LLM output into a repeatable artifact

The project is designed as an engineering system, not a one-off notebook.

Structured validation

A local 35B model can return malformed JSON or partial schema matches. The evaluator wraps every response in Pydantic validation, explicit parse exceptions, raw-response logging, and retry-aware batch execution.

Streaming and concurrency

Large JSONL outputs are loaded and aggregated with streaming readers. Concurrent evaluation is handled with ThreadPoolExecutor, bounded workers, and resumable per-year output files.

Publication checks

The current suite has 386 automated tests. The public report is rebuilt from source data and checked with a leakage scanner before publication.

Failure mode Guardrail Evidence
Malformed LLM JSON Pydantic validation, explicit parse errors, raw-response logging, and retry-aware execution. Schema compliance improved from 62% to 100% on the sampled response set.
Memory pressure Streaming JSONL readers replace full-list loading for yearly evaluation outputs. Peak footprint dropped from 4.2GB to 0.3GB on the largest evaluated year.
Interrupted batch runs Per-year outputs, bounded workers, and resumable report assembly keep long runs inspectable. The public page is rebuilt from generated artifacts, not edited by hand.
Public data leakage Company names and source excerpts are withheld from the public case file. A leakage scanner runs before publication and blocks unsafe HTML.

Rebuild path

uv run pytest
uv run python scripts/build_report.py
uv run python scripts/check_leakage.py output/report/index.html

System boundary

Inspect how evaluation modules depend on config, parsing, validation, and batch orchestration.
